commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6573602b93104e8f2c5398a6db4e0b775d0790f6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR caps `acquireSessionWriteLock`'s effective `maxHoldMs` by the acquire timeout and adds lock-payload tests for default, low explicit, and high explicit values.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +86. Total +105 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main resolves the default acquire timeout to 60000 ms and `maxHoldMs` to 300000 ms, so a legal holder can outlive waiters. I did not run a live contending gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers are a compatibility-sensitive `maxHoldMs` policy decision and contributor-provided real contention proof, not a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(session-write-lock): bound maxHoldMs by acquire timeout
target.body:
## Summary

The session write lock's `maxHoldMs` defaulted to 300 s (5 min) regardless of the acquire `timeoutMs` (60 s). This created a situation where a plugin hook (e.g. `active-memory` sub-agent in `before_prompt_build`) could hold the lock for the full 5 min while other operations timed out after 60 s, producing `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` for every concurrent operation that shares the same lock file (e.g. `mirrorCodexAppServerTranscript`).

The fix bounds `maxHoldMs` in `acquireSessionWriteLock` by `timeoutMs` (using `resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout` with `minMs: 1, graceMs: 1`), so a holder cannot block other acquirers past their configured acquire timeout. Explicit `maxHoldMs` values below the bound are still honored; values above the bound are capped.

## Changes

- `src/agents/session-write-lock.ts`: in `acquireSessionWriteLock`, compute `maxHoldFromTimeout = resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout({ timeoutMs, minMs: 1, graceMs: 1 })` and use `Math.min(resolvedMaxHoldMs, maxHoldFromTimeout)` as the effective hold limit. With default 60 s timeout, `maxHoldMs` is now ~60 s instead of 300 s.
- `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`: added three tests:
  - Default timeout (60 s) → maxHoldMs bounded to ≤ 60001 ms
  - Explicit low maxHoldMs (5000) → preserved at 5000
  - Explicit high maxHoldMs (500000) → capped to ≤ 60001 ms

## Reproduction

Before fix — lock file shows `maxHoldMs: 300000` even with 60 s timeout:
```json
{"pid": 1302, "createdAt": "2026-06-24T16:31:53.605Z", "maxHoldMs": 300000}
```

After fix — lock file shows `maxHoldMs: 60001` matching the 60 s acquire timeout:
```json
{"pid": 1302, "createdAt": "2026-06-24T16:31:53.605Z", "maxHoldMs": 60001}
```

## Proof

Runtime verification (tsx, Node 24):

### A. Lock payload records bounded maxHoldMs
```
Lock payload: {"pid":947139,"createdAt":"2026-06-24T20:29:13.525Z","maxHoldMs":60001,"starttime":8425133}
maxHoldMs: 60001
Bounded: YES
```

### B. Contending acquire succeeds after holder releases
```
Holder acquired lock (maxHoldMs bounded to ~5000ms)
Holder releasing lock at ~2001ms
Waiter acquired lock at ~2043ms
PASS: Waiter succeeded within timeout
```

### C. Explicit low maxHoldMs preserved
```
Configured maxHoldMs: 2000
Recorded maxHoldMs: 2000
PASS: YES
```

### D. Explicit high maxHoldMs capped
```
Configured maxHoldMs: 500000
Recorded maxHoldMs: 60001 (capped at timeoutMs)
PASS: YES
```
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
